item.title: Autumn Dragon: China-nexus APT Group Targets South East Asia
item.description: In this report, we describe how we tracked for several months a sustained espionage campaign against the government, media, and news sectors in several countries including Laos, Cambodia, Singapore, the Philippines and Indonesia

item.title: TG-VoIP-001: Cryptocurrency-Based VoIP Infrastructure Promoted via Telegram
item.description: Our threat hunting team uncovered a suspicious VoIP/SIP infrastructure tied to malicious activity on Telegram’s marketplace. This network advertises IP telephony for call centers but exhibits hallmarks of gray-market operations using cryptocurrency

item.title: How Fraudsters Use Phishing Campaigns for Account Takeovers
item.description: Ranging from 419 scams to sophisticated spear-phishing schemes, phishing is a prevalent social engineering technique employed by hackers to illicitly obtain employees’ credentials, users’ passwords, or credit card information.

item.title: BidenCash Dump 2.1M Compromised Debit/Credit Cards
item.description: On February 28th '23, BidenCash, a card shop, celebrated their first anniversary by uploading over two million stolen debit/credit cards onto the XSS dark web forum. The data dump consisted of the compromised card numbers and personal information.

item.title: No Honor Among Thieves Pt 2: Malware Dropper Distribution Via Malware Stealer Logs
item.description: This article examines the tactics employed by cybercriminals to distribute malware droppers via malware stealer logs within the dark web community. These logs are then shared with other cybercriminals, who then unknowingly infect themselves.

item.title: No Honor Among Thieves Pt 1: Reverse-Engineering A Ransomware Decryption Tool
item.description: A look at two cases that show how paying a ransom often fails to solve a ransomware victim’s problems because there is no honor among thieves; moreover, perpetrators are financially motivated to not hold up their end of the bargain.

item.title: SUNBURST Malware: Backdoor State and C2
item.description: On December 13th, FireEye published a reportdetailing threat actors employing SolarWinds supply chain to compromise various victims globally with the SUNBURST backdoor. Our findings for this backdoor are detailed in this article.

item.title: 2020 Fraud Summary For The Financial Sector
item.description: Cybercriminals are employing new tactics and techniques to stay ahead of the mitigations put into place by financial institutions. They are exploiting vulnerabilities, researching new tactics and developing novel attack vectors.

item.title: How a Bot Engine's Backdoor is Used to Deliver Nemesis Stealer
item.description: DarkArmor recently analyzed a malware dropper that dropped a malware downloader called Bot Engine, which then downloaded a recent Nemesis stealer. This report describes the Bot Engine framework and how it is used to infect users.

item.title: BidenCash Dump 1.9M Compromised Debit/Credit Cards
item.description: On December 20th, 2023, BidenCash, a card shop, uploaded and shared 1.9 million stolen debit/credit cards on the XSS dark web forum. The data dump consisted of compromised card numbers, expiration dates, and CVV numbers.

item.title: Unveiling the Latest Microsoft Teams Phishing Campaign Targeting Small Businesses
item.description: In this blog post, we will delve into the details of the latest Microsoft Teams phishing campaign, exploring its tactics, techniques, and procedures, and providing crucial insights to help small businesses stay vigilant.

item.title: Cyber Fraud Fusion Operation: The Rise of Next-Gen Security Operations Centers (SOCs)
item.description: Welcome to the new era of fraud detection and prevention, where Next-Gen SOCs seamlessly integrate with the Fraud team, collectively serving as the frontline defenders against an increasingly sophisticated array of cyber threats and frauds.

item.title: The Rise of a New Dark Web Market
item.description: The darkweb marketplace landscape underwent a significant shift several years ago. The advent of secure chat services such as Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp introduced a new dynamic. As a result, marketplaces operated by fraudsters began to emerge.

item.title: Vietnamese Phishers Target Meta Business Owners with Over 400 Phish Pages
item.description: On November 23, 2024, our researchers uncovered a phishing campaign targeting Meta business account owners. Vietnamese threat actors, referred to as quyetnd288, created over 400 phishing sites aimed at stealing login credentials.

item.title: Understanding Coinbase Account Checking
item.description: Account checking, also known as credential stuffing or account validation, is a tactic used by cybercriminals to verify whether certain accounts exist on a platform, typically depending on automated scripts and software.

item.title: Investment Scam: The Operations
item.description: An analysis of an active investment scam targeting individuals through online platforms. The scheme is disguised as either a job opportunity or a high-yield investment and uses sophisticated social engineering and cryptocurrency transactions.

item.title: Investment Scam Playbook: From Job Recruitment To Investment Scam
item.description: Scams have evolved from basic phishing emails to sophisticated multi-stage operations that prey on individuals’ vulnerabilities. The “Investment Scam” starts with an unsuspecting job recruitment message and culminates in devastating financial loss.

item.title: Inside a Malware Campaign: A Nigerian Hacker’s Perspective
item.description: On 02/07/2025, our platform obtained various screenshots taken from a malware stealer (infostealer) showing a cybercriminal, whom we believe to be from Nigeria (based on his IP address), performing various activities in a malware campaign.

item.title: Phishing Attack Targets Canadian Infrastructure on Canada Day
item.description: Our systems detected a coordinated phishing campaign leveraging PDF-themed lures to harvest user email credentials. The attackers used socially engineered emails that mimicked service notifications, account updates, and login requests.

item.title: Threat Insight: Cybercriminals Abusing Vercel to Deliver Remote Access Malware
item.description: CyberArmor has identified a phishing campaign leveraging Vercel, a legitimate frontend hosting platform, to distribute a malicious version of LogMeIn, a legitimate remote access tool, by cybercriminals to gain full control over victims’ machines.

item.title: Uncovered: How Cybercriminals Scam Each Other Using Fake USDT Sender Software
item.description: See how cybercriminals weaponized "FLASH USDT Sender" software. Our analysis reveals it's a sophisticated dropper that installs a suite of malware—including a stealer, clipper, and RDP grabber—for complete system control.

item.title: GraphQL Phishing: Gmail-Themed Credential Harvesting Behind Cloudflare
Exploring the Evolution of Online Criminal Activity: A Dive into Modern Cybercrime
item.description: Cybercrime has evolved from isolated individuals to international networks. In this paper we delves into this evolution, highlighting the complexities of modern cybercriminal operations and the challenges they pose to law enforcement.

item.title: Account Takeover Through OTP Bots: The Latest Cyber Threat
item.description: Threats have evolved at an alarming rate, with attackers constantly finding new ways to exploit vulnerabilities and compromise user accounts. One is account takeover through OTP bots, posing a significant risk to individuals and businesses alike.

item.title: Global Phishing Targeting Meta Business Accounts
item.description: Our threat intelligence team investigated a phishing campaign impersonating Meta Business and Ads Manager support, using fake “policy violation” and “trademark rights” alerts to steal credentials, hijack ad accounts, and launch fraudulent campaigns

item.title: White Snake: Latest Campaign With Multi-Stage Malware Dropper
item.description: Threat actors are constantly developing sophisticated methods to compromise systems. One of the latest threats is the Tax Invoice-themed campaign, which employs a multi-stage malware dropper to deliver the WhiteSnake malware stealer.

item.title: New North Korean-Based Backdoor
item.description: In this paper we analyze a new threat campaign which is specifically focused on Aerospace and Defense companies: sectors appealing to multiple threat actors, but of particular interest to North Korean threat groups in other recent campaigns.

item.title: 2022 Fraud Summary For The Financial Sector
item.description: In 2022, DarkArmor uncovered 9,573 accounts being sold on the darkweb with the sum of $683 million. These accounts were openly available for sale and the cybercriminals even provided online banking screenshots as proof.

item.title: Trademark Scams: Application Approval Scams Using Caller ID Spoofing
item.description: We deep dive into trademark application scams where the scammers impersonates the USPTO (United States Patent and Trademark Office) and spoofs their phone number to lure an applicant to pay additional fees to the scammer.

item.title: How a Nigerian Hacker Uses AI to Build Cybercrime Tools and Launch Phishing Attacks
item.description: Our platform collected and analyzed over 3,000 screenshots from a Nigerian-based cybercriminal, giving a special behind-the-scenes look into the daily operations of a modern threat actor, revealing how campaigns are built with the help of AI.

item.title: Hacker Exploits Social Security Statement Theme to Target Over 2,000 Victims with Malware
item.description: In a recent campaign uncovered by CyberArmor analysts, cybercriminals exploited the official them of the Social Security Administration (SSA) in a convincing phishing lure that deceived over 2,000 individuals into downloading malware.

item.title: Autumn Dragon: China-nexus APT Group Targets South East Asia
Device Authentication Phishing: Account Takeover via Microsoft Device Code Flow
Operation Gauloises: How One Operator Bridges the DarkWeb to the Levant via Whish Money
